item.title: Basketball is stuck, cheerleader to the rescue
item.dc:creator: Grogu007

item.title: Proud Progress Pic! From needing a mobility aid to swole.
item.description: Little context, I've needed a mobility walker for quite some time, because of my hyper mobility, Lupus and MS. but I decided to hit the gym, mostly for my mental health because of family problems. I now only need a walking stick to get about for the most part, still need the walker for longer days, but I'm very proud of where I've got to.

item.dc:creator: strongp

item.title: Unsolved crime
item.description: I hate seeing the elderly disrespected and victimized. One day we’ll reach that age and our deeds may come back to us. Reminds me of a scene from the Honeymooners I’ve never forgotten— RALPH: “I’ve always followed that old adage: Be kind to people you meet on the way up, because you’re going to meet the same people on the way down.” ED: “Happens to me every day in the sewer.”

item.title: I'm sorry, WHAT?!
item.description: The ONLY footage I have ever seen of an agitated/aggressive capybara. I shouldn't be shocked... But I'm shocked.
item.dc:creator: GhostTater

item.title: Carefully curated,easily-read Ukraine news w. sources,for those lacking time but seeking info. Big dump today.
item.description:
Over 400 total people have been taken hostage. They were even firing at windows in the hospital in an attempt to elicit a response from Ukrainian defense forces, outing their positions. https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60757133https://www.foxnews.com/world/mariupol-hospital-occupied-russian-forces-report https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-03-16/ukraine-says-russia-holds-400-hospital-patients-staff-hostage/100912918 https://www.foxnews.com/world/mariupol-hospital-occupied-russian-forces-report https://www.axios.com/ukraine-russia-mariupol-hospital-hostages-2d387184-0541-4afe-a260-89e1f2151539.html
They made this half-assed excuse, saying they've stopped advertising/investments in russia: https://www.nestle.com/ask-nestle/our-company/answers/update-russia-ukraine Boycott the shit out of these profiteering whores and expose/pressure them. https://www.businessinsider.com/ukraine-prime-minister-calls-nestle-stop-all-business-russia-2022-3 https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2022-03-17/ukraine-prime-minister-urges-nestle-to-halt-business-in-russia https://www.newsweek.com/nestles-russia-ties-puts-company-twitter-users-crosshairs-1689068
The counter-offensive reclaims territory as Ukrainian forces continue to fight towards Kherson, where they've made artillery bombardments and air strikes recently, destroying several russian helicopters. Refer to my last UA post for details on russian losses. Note: You are obliged to take the accuracy of this claim with a Grain of Salt­™ – reasoning is that the single source below is the only actual *publication* I can find that has formally reported the ~75 miles estimate. My *actual* sources on this are from personal accounts with 10+ years of internet activity that I can verify are not propaganda/troll accounts. I hope when we wake up tomorrow that we have more sources verifying it. https://www.dailykos.com/stories/2022/3/17/2086493/-Ukraine-update-Ukraine-retakes-75-miles-of-Russian-held-territory-pushes-toward-Kherson
I'd previously posted about the PMs of Poland, Czech Republic, and Slovenia visiting Kyiv by train, which took iron balls in and of itself – this is likely the reason, and the countries that would be involved. Poland's involvement is both the most likely and most significant. https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-polytics/3432515-presidents-office-ukraine-wants-to-end-war-by-forming-coalition-able-to-stop-russia-in-future.html
To be more specific, a tele-meeting, not an in-person face-to-face meeting.NOTE: While I've been sent a couple video clips of broadcasts, but other then the link below, I do not have more sources for this. It's hard for news agencies to make a report solely to say another news agency started reporting something they already knew to be true. https://uscnpm.org/2022/03/12/hu-wei-russia-ukraine-war-china-choice/
It's basically more MAD/Nuclear threats, but their continues to blame western powers for their invasion somehow – an absolutely idiotic rhetoric. They accuse the U.S. of "toking a wild Russophobic plot to tear Russia apart". https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russia-will-put-its-enemies-such-united-states-their-place-medvedev-says-2022-03-17/ https://www.express.co.uk/news/world/1582191/Russia-news-usa-putin-biden-superpower-nuclear-ukraine-war-threat
NATO has already vowed that violations of airspace and attacks "even if accidental" on any NATO states would invoke the 5th clause and trigger a defensive-only war against russia, but have not responded to the ongoing violations of airspace and even a crashed drone carrying a russian bomb in Croatia. https://www.cnn.com/2022/03/17/politics/us-nato-errant-drones-unintentional-conflict-russia-ukraine/index.html https://abcnews.go.com/International/wireStory/official-drone-crashed-croatia-carried-bomb-83421615https://goodwordnews.com/wired-so-tight-right-now-how-the-us-and-nato-are-preparing-against-errant-drones-and-the-unintended-conflict-with-russia/ https://www.pehalnews.in/drones-flying-into-nato-territory-have-forced-the-alliance-to-decide-how-to-respond-if-at-all-to-incidents-inside-its-borders/1767037/
What's amazing is that we can literally watch this in real time: https://www.marinetraffic.com/en/ais/home/centerx:31.5/centery:45.6/zoom:9 Technology is amazing, even if it often serves only to expose us to this kind of fuckery.
This is per the estimates of Mariupol Deputy Mayor Serhiy Orlov and his officials. https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2022/03/17/7332133/ https://kyivindependent.com/uncategorized/official-says-80-90-of-mariupol-destroyed-or-damaged-by-russian-shelling-airstrikes/
It may seem like a silly gesture, given that UA has much bigger concerns at the moment, but it means more than just that. Not only does Italy has extensive experience restoring and rebuilding even ancient monuments with perfect accuracy, but this is a statement of postwar solidarity. Italy (as well as, likely, most of Europe) will help UA rebuild once the war is over and russia is thrown out. https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/italy-ready-rebuild-theatre-ukraines-mariupol-minister-2022-03-17/ https://kyivindependent.com/uncategorized/italy-is-ready-to-rebuild-mariupol-theater-destroyed-by-the-russian-military/ https://www.newsweek.com/italy-says-it-will-help-rebuild-mariupols-theater-after-russian-bombing-1689306 https://twitter.com/dariofrance/status/1504499779136544775
The source is from UK intelligence, but corroborated by other intelligence communities as well. The general consensus among the russian military is that it is now impossible to take Kyiv using traditional land-based forces. At face value, this sounds like a victory... And it very well may be one, but there is still a significant possibility that this means they will simply increase the frequency, scale, potency, and brutality of air/artillery strikes. Occupation is one thing, indiscriminate killings are another. https://inews.co.uk/news/politics/ukraine-war-russians-could-be-set-to-abandon-plans-for-ground-assault-on-kyiv-uk-intelligence-suggests-1524490?ITO=newsnow https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/europe/ukraine-russia-war-putin-zelensky-peace-latest-b2038633.html
It's no secret that Ukraine is the "bread basket" of Europe, but that's an understatement; they more wheat than any other country in the world, not to mention many other significant exports, like the iconic sunflowers. Farmers are seriously struggling, for obvious reasons... But they are still attempting to sow for the coming season. There is still significant doubt regarding Ukrainian farming come summer and fall. Despite their efforts, there is almost certainly going to be significant famine in the coming year as a result. https://farmpolicynews.illinois.edu/2022/03/ukraine-planting-farmers-are-pushing-forward-wherever-possible/ https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2022-03-04/war-hit-crop-superpower-ukraine-faces-struggle-to-even-sow https://www.agricensus.com/Article/ANALYSIS-Ukraine-planting-outlooks-slashed-amid-Russia-invasion-21174.html
Some of the sabotage was physical, but there were cyber-attacks as well. In the case of the former, at least, it is suspected to be at the hands of Belarusian anti-war activists. Glory to them as well! The cyber attack components were likely not local, at least not in their entirety. https://www.voiceofbelarus.org/belarus-news/the-rail-war-and-rail-partisans-in-belarus/ https://www.railfreight.com/railfreight/2022/02/28/belarussian-railways-allegedly-hacked-again-to-slow-troop-transport/ https://www.jpost.com/breaking-news/article-699184
They don't have the resources or time to do these poor dead citizens the honor of proper burial. I myself have seen videos of the dead... Not just young freedom fighters, but old men, pregnant women, babes, etc. I will not share these videos in my curations of news, though a standalone post for russian war crimes may be in the future. The links below do contain graphic content, though... Tame compared to the uncensored truth. https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60729206 https://apnews.com/article/russia-ukraine-mariupol-mass-grave-af9477cd69d067c34e0e336c05d765cc https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-10624247/Corpses-dumped-mass-graves-Ukraine-town-dozens-killed-Russian-troops.html https://abcnews.go.com/International/wireStory/amid-heavy-shelling-ukraines-mariupol-city-mass-grave-83361805
I promise to keep making these posts. I may also be posting some documentation of russian war crimes so far. If I do, such a post will be exceedingly graphic. I need to check exactly what imgur will allow, and may have to only share links to external sites with disclaimers. This post came in late, so the new day has already begun, meaning the content of this post does not include the latest from contacts and from credible news sources for this day (18/03/2022). These posts pertain mostly to the 16th and 17th. That is all for now, imgur. Good night. SLAVA UKRAINI! PUTAIN KHULYO!!!
item.dc:creator: VikingWeightlifting

item.title: geez, I wonder what was in the data set they used to train it
item.dc:creator: piconuke

item.title: 150+ REJECTIONS from publishers
item.description: First published book just arrived in the mail. I've been self-publishing since 2012, and have had 150+ rejections from publishers over the last decade. Now in 2022 my books will be available in Barnes & Noble, and other locations across Canada and the US. This is surreal. I ugly-cried as soon as this video ended LOL I saw a Gary Vee quote where he said this is a 4 quarter game, and at the age of 46, he feels like he's in the 2nd quarter. If you've been working on a project and you're not getting the results you want, if you feel like it's taking too long ... keep working. Keep trying new things. DEFINITELY keep knocking on new doors, because to succeed, only one has to open. Apologies for the self indulgence, I just wanted to share with Imgurians. Love you guys. xox PS: I didn't film this myself so technically it's not a selfie, I hope I didn't break the iron-clad 'no selfie rule' at Imgur :P PPS: I'm fully prepared for the 'girls showing object with her face next to it' meme, so have at it LOL PPPS: I won't post a book description or any other crap on here, but if you want to see the cover close-up (Isaac Escorza IS F'ING AMAZING) or read the book summary, you can click here http://arenamode.com xo
item.dc:creator: BlakeNorthcott

item.title: May God protect you!
item.description: Today the servicemen of 72 OMBr named after Black Cossacks. Victoria and Sergei met together not so long ago, when the Russian occupier began a full-scale invasion of our state. Without a wedding bouquet, high heels and a white dress, in this difficult time, almost on the front line, two destinies, two hearts united, a new family was created! We wish the newlyweds a happy family life! May God protect you! Glory to Ukraine!
item.dc:creator: EnderTheTurk
